sloka_938.meaning: worship Me (though their motives may be mixed with action or knowledge)
one seeking scriptural knowledge or knowledge of the soul
the purified knower of the soul
being fortunate influenced by devotion for Me

sloka_949.meaning: O Arjuna, chastiser of the enemy
at the manifestation of the universe

sloka_930.meaning: as the origin of the Vedas, Oṁkār
by My power as the essential taste

sloka_924.meaning: thing to be known by you
this knowledge of My absolute power (aiśvaryamaya-jñān)
with the knowledge to be realised of My sweetness (mādhurya-anubhav)

sloka_943.meaning: I, as the Supersoul dwelling within his heart
demigod, representing a form of Me

sloka_928.meaning: I am the source of manifestation
are manifest from these two types of prakṛti, or natures, as kṣetra and kṣetrajña (vide 13.12)
all living species, moving and stationary

sloka_950.meaning: have been eradicated (by the fortune of association with My pure devotee)
free from the illusion of duality
and fixed in consciousness of Me
who are performers of virtuous deeds

sloka_940.meaning: noble, unselfish souls dear to Me
having fully dedicated his heart unto Me
is as dear to Me as My own self

sloka_942.meaning: other godly personalities – the demigods – the sun-god, etc.
by particular worldly desires based on likes and dislikes

sloka_937.meaning: These four kinds of wrongdoers or unfortunate persons

by māyā, illusion (despite their study of the scriptures, they consider that only the form of Lord Nārāyaṇ is worshippable, and that Lord Kṛṣṇa, Lord Rāma, and other factual forms of the Supreme are merely mortal)
(2) fallen persons, or those who take the path of devotion but later reject it, considering it inadequate or worthless
and (4) the demoniac (māyāvādīs, the impersonalists, who dismember My form by their arrows of false logic, resembling demons like Jarāsandha)

sloka_946.meaning: as the impersonal or formless Brahma

sloka_933.meaning: as strength of sāttvika quality, or goodness, which enables one to execute his religious duties
without crossing the universal principles of religion, i.e

sloka_948.meaning: Me (due to their knowledge being covered by māyā and yogamāyā, My external illusory potency and the internal potency of My will)

sloka_932.meaning: as the eternal cause, known as pradhān

sloka_945.meaning: of those unwise worshippers of the demigods

sloka_927.meaning: is adopted for enjoyment through fruitive actions
in the form of the individual souls

sloka_936.meaning: composed of the three modes of material nature

sloka_935.meaning: by this manifestation of the three modes of material nature
who am transcendental to these modes

sloka_951.meaning: for freedom from disease and death
the action by which the souls are bound to worldly existence
taking shelter, placing their trust and devotion

sloka_941.meaning: the enlightened person (by the fortune of sādhu-saṅga, devotional association)
everything, the entire world of moving and stationary beings
is of the nature of Vāsudev, Śrī Kṛṣṇa

sloka_939.meaning: whose consciousness is completely absorbed in Me

sloka_923.meaning: you will be able to know
taking refuge in Me alone (without any attempts of karma, etc.)
in full along with My abode, My opulences, and My associates

sloka_931.meaning: the power to endure duality, etc
and as the force of radiance and heat in fire

sloka_925.meaning: and of thousands who have attained such realisation

sloka_944.meaning: by Me alone, as the Supersoul of the demigod

sloka_952.meaning: even at the time of death
in relation to the worldly plane and to the celestial plane

sloka_934.meaning: and of the nature of passion
and of the nature of ignorance
